message:
perf(mvcc): thread cached chain_len from publish through post-commit pruning

Follow-up to the prior enforce_chain_bound_for_page → Result<usize>
change. With the pre-publish chain length now available, thread it
through the whole commit path so post_commit_version_maintenance can
skip its own chain_length() traversals too.

publish_write_set signature:
  Result<CommitSeq>
  → Result<(CommitSeq, SmallVec<[usize; 8]>)>

SmallVec<[usize; 8]> keeps the common case (≤8 written pages per txn)
fully on the stack; larger write-sets spill once to the heap.

post_commit_version_maintenance now takes
  pre_publish_chain_lens: &[usize]
and its maybe_prune closure picks the cached value by page index,
saturating_add(1) to reflect that publish appended the new version.
If the caller omits the cache (slice shorter than pages), we fall
back to version_store.chain_length(pgno) — keeps the function safe
against future callers that don't have the cache.

Also drops the trailing version_store.advance_epoch() call from
post_commit_version_maintenance: epoch advancement is already
covered by the MVCC lifecycle silo advancer that runs on
asupersync, so the unconditional bump here was redundant and
occasionally caused double-epoch spikes under contention.
